summary: Markets snapped this week under the weight of two converging forces: the U.S.-Iran war keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, and a Fed that raised its inflation outlook but refused to signal cuts. Stocks and bonds sold off in tandem, gold posted its worst week since 1983, and rate futures briefly priced a coin-flip chance the Fed's next move is a hike. A hotter-than-expected February PPI — up 0.
tweet: The Fed killed any remaining hope of a 2026 cut after Powell acknowledged "zero" net job growth yet called conditions "solid" — leaving markets to absorb an oil shock with no policy relief in sight.
